definition: A sovereign downgrade below AA would trigger significant regulatory and plumbing consequences through three channels (Basel III risk-weight, repo eligibility/haircuts, insurance/pension mandates), but the binding threshold is more distant than initially presented — AA+ to AA does NOT cross the Basel III 0% to 20% risk-weight threshold; only A+ would.
evidence: Current ratings: S&P AA+, Fitch AA+, Moody's Aa1 (downgraded May 2025) — two notches above binding threshold
Basel III risk-weight change (0% to 20%) triggered only at A+ or below
2011 template (S&P AAA to AA+) was benign because it stayed above regulatory thresholds
Japan serial downgrades (Aaa to A1) produced negligible impact due to ~90% domestic ownership and regulatory accommodation
U.S. ~30-40% foreign holdings makes Japan pathway partially unavailable
open_questions: At what rating level would repo eligibility and insurance mandate restrictions become binding?
Could regulatory accommodation preempt mechanical consequences of a downgrade?
